data.title: Addnode Group’s Annual Report 2024 published
data.description:
Addnode Group has today published the Annual Report 2024. The report shows how Addnode Group accelerates sustainable value growth by continuously acquiring new businesses and actively support its subsidiaries to drive organic growth.

Net sales for 2024 increased by 5 percent to SEK 7,757 m. During the year, the company's partner Autodesk changed its transaction model from a reseller model to an agent model. Acquired growth would have been 10 percent and organic currency-adjusted growth would have been 9 percent with the previous Autodesk reseller model and before reclassifications of third-party agreement.

As a result of organic growth, improved efficiency and complementary acquisitions, EBITA increased by 35 percent to SEK 863 m. At the same time, the EBITA margin improved to 11.1 percent (8.6) and earnings per share increased by 44 percent to 3.02 SEK.

“Despite a challenging environment, both geopolitically and market-wise, all divisions delivered their best results ever. By investing in product development, launching new digital solutions, attracting new customers and carrying out acquisitions, Addnode Group remained an entrepreneur-driven and forward-leaning corporate group,” Johan Andersson, Addnode Group’s President and CEO, writes in the report.

Some highlights from 2024:

  • Seven acquisitions with a combined net sales of approximately SEK 155 m have been completed during the year: Efficture (Sweden), Jetas Quality Systems (Sweden), Optimec (Canada), GPS Timber (Sweden), Addoceo (Sweden), Prime Aerostructures (Austria) and CTC Software (USA).
  • The sister companies Sokigo and S-GROUP Solutions merged in order to clarify our offering in digital solutions for sustainable community development. The merged company now operates under the name Sokigo and has around 250 employees.
  • The offering to the forestry sector in the subsidiary Sokigo was transformed into a separate company under the name Icebound. The new company consists of around 30 employees.
  • Symetri's partner Autodesk implemented a new transaction model that decreases net sales and purchases of goods and services, and is replaced by a commission income that corresponds to the gross margin. Gross profit and EBITA are unchanged while margins increase. Symetri continues to be the one who, together with the customer, identifies and implements the best solution.
  • A new brand identity was launched. The new visual expression of the identity and its associated new purpose, “Digitalization for a Better Society”, provide a strong platform for the further growth and globalization of Addnode Group.

The report is attached with this press release and is also available at https://www.addnodegroup.com/investors/reports-and-presentations/, where the Swedish version of the report also is available in European Single Electronic Format (ESEF).

data.title: Oil companies slip on lower crude prices
data.description:
** Shares of oil companies fell in premarket trading following prices of crude

** Brent futures fall 3.5% to $67.69/bbl; U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures decline 3.8% to $64.43/bbl by 0926 GMT - both on course for their biggest weekly losses in percentage terms for half a year

** Crude weighed down by hit from U.S. President Donald Trump's barrage of new tariffs and output increases announced by the OPEC+ producer group

** U.S. oil majors Exxon Mobil down 1.7%, while Chevron falls 1.5%

** Producers Occidental Petroleum down 1.4%, Permian Resources slips 1.7%, while oilfield services company Halliburton declines 2.6% and top oilfield co SLB down 1.2%

** Europe's oil cos (.SXEP) down ~3%, with BP slipping 2.1% and Shell falling 2.6%

data.title: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) prices remain stable in Europe
data.description: This week, PVC prices rolled over in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "The European polyvinyl chloride spot market exhibited stability during the week as participants awaited the effects of the ethylene MCP settlement on PVC pricing. Attention shifted towards the European ethylene industry's settled contract price for April, which showed a decrease compared to March. Average naphtha spot prices for March also declined from the previous month's figures. In the PVC contract market, most stakeholders adopted a cautious approach, monitoring how the drop in ethylene prices would influence PVC costs. A few producers were reported to be negotiating at a rollover from March, citing difficulties in maintaining producer margins. While some sources predicted a reduction from March, they acknowledged the current uncertainty in making accurate forecasts. Demand throughout March and into early April mirrored that of January and February, with only a modest uptick in seasonal demand from the construction sector. Although buying interest from the construction industry in Spain was stronger than in other parts of Europe, one source noted a slight deceleration in orders from Spanish construction clients. Across Europe, expectations were for a slight decline in demand in April due to the Easter holidays. Interest in materials from Asia persisted, but some market participants hesitated to place orders until they had clearer insights into April's contract prices. One source suggested that if producers maintained firm pricing, it could result in an increase in imports from Asia. Imports from Mexico were reported to continue throughout March and into April, with normal availability anticipated to resume in May. The source added, "Supply in Europe continues to be abundant, despite the ongoing and scheduled maintenance activities in the region and a decrease in imports. This situation is largely influenced by diminished demand from key customers and sectors. Last week, a suspension of operations at an s-PVC production facility in Poland was lifted. However, PVC production units in Belgium faced challenges in their integrated chlorine cell room, resulting in reduced production rates since last week. Although plans for a restart are in progress, the duration of this issue remains uncertain. Additionally, a PVC production unit in Germany is set for maintenance in April, lasting one month, while maintenance in the Netherlands has been extended by an additional week beyond its initial six-week schedule. This week, spot PVC prices have remained stable, with import offers primarily coming from China, Taiwan, and South Korea. However, interest in purchasing imports is limited due to the overall decline in PVC demand across the region and decreased offtake from major converters. In the spot markets, FD North West Europe PVC prices were assessed at the Euro 875-885/mt levels, left unchanged from last week.In the contract markets, PVC Suspension grade prices were assessed at the Euro 990-995/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE France levels, both steady week on week. PVC Suspension grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1000-1005/mt FD NWE Italy levels, unchanged from last week. Meanwhile, PVC prices were assessed at the GBP 830-835/mt FD NWE UK levels, up GBP (+5/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, feedstock EDC prices were assessed at the USD 175-185/mt FOB North West Europe levels while VCM prices were assessed at the USD 525-535/mt FOB North West Europe levels, both week on week decreased by USD (-10/mt).Ethylene spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the Euro 840-850/mt FD North West Europe levels, marginally down Euro (-5/mt) from the previous week.European Ethylene contract price for April 2025 settled at the Euro 1205/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a plunge of Euro 55/MT from its March 2025 settlement levels. levels.

data.title: SocGen's Overnight Economic News Summary
data.description:
Societe Generale in its early Friday economic news summary pointed out:

— VIX up to 30. 10-year United States Treasury below 4.0%, interim projection of H/S is 3.86%. Bund drops below 2.60%, filling March gap — German spending plan. President Trump downplays sell-off in equities, says open to tariff reductions if countries "can offer something phenomenal."

— Day ahead: Fed Chair Powell speaks on economy with Q&A. U.S. nonfarm payrolls (NFP) SocGen forecasts up 170,000, whisper 120,000 gains, consensus up 140,000, range 80,000-200,000. Unemployment rate unchanged at 4.1%, earnings +0.3% month over month. Canada employment (LFS).

— Swiss franc, yen, euro, South Korean won and Brazil's real best performers this week in foreign exchange, Australian dollar and South Africa's rand (domestic political turmoil) are the main laggards.

— German factory orders flat month over month in February (-0.2% year over year), consensus forecast was for 3.4% gain. Domestic -1.2%, foreign +0.8%.

— Sweden's consumer price index slows to 0.5% year over year in March from 1.3% in February, below forecast. CPIF eases to 2.3% from 2.9%.

— Nikkei -2.5%, EUR 10-year IRS -4bps at 2.515%, Brent crude -0.8% at $69.6/barrel, Gold -0.1% at $3,105/oz.

data.title: Don't forget about payrolls
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 down 2.3%
  • Europe's banks down 7%
  • Investors await payrolls
  • Wall St futures lower

DON'T FORGET ABOUT PAYROLLS

While all eyes, understandably, have been on Trump's tariffs this week, markets have one more key risk event to come: the U.S. jobs report.

Economists surveyed by Reuters expect the U.S. to have added 135,000 nonfarm payrolls in March, down from the 151,000 added in February. Estimates range from a low of 50,000 (KPMG) to a high of 185,000 (Bank of America).

BofA is expecting "some payback" in leisure and hospitality jobs that were lost in January and February on cold weather, while they expect "minimal" impact from DOGE cuts so far.

KPMG are of a different view.

"We know that at least 24,000 federal workers were laid off due to their probationary status and may have not been on federal payrolls through March 12 in order to be counted as employed during the month," they write.

"Over time, we tend to lose two private sector workers for every federal job cut due to spillover effects on contractors and the ecosystem of communities."

So what does it mean for the Fed?

RBC BlueBay Asset Management chief investment officer Mark Dowding believes the Fed will be on hold "for the foreseeable futures" as long as there is not a large rise in unemployment, which could trigger recession fears.

The unemployment rate is expected to remain steady at 4.1%.

(Samuel Indyk)

*****

FRIDAY'S OTHER LIVE MARKETS POSTS:

BANKS LEAD EUROPEAN SHARES LOWER, STOXX SET FOR BIGGEST WEEKLY FALL IN THREE YEARS CLICK HERE

EUROPE BEFORE THE BELL: FUTURES EDGE DOWN AFTER THURSDAY'S SHARP SELL-OFF CLICK HERE

WALL STREET HAS MOST TO LOSE FROM TRUST LOST CLICK HERE

data.title: Puma's CEO Change Seems Positive Amid Doubts on Current StrategyMarket Talk
data.description: Puma's CEO change seems a positive step for the company, given investors had lost some faith in the current strategy, Deutsche Bank analysts write in a note. The German sporting-goods group named Arthur Hoeld as its new CEO, replacing Arne Freundt, who will leave due to differing views with the supervisory board on strategy execution. "We believe a new CEO can change the internal attitude in the company relatively quickly," they say. Hoeld's strategic direction might focus on accepting Puma's lower price position in the market, Deutsche says. However, the timing is unfortunate as the group has to navigate the impact of U.S. tariffs, which are likely to put material pressure on earnings momentum, the analysts say. Shares fall 1.7%. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Bonds Rally as Investors Continue to Hide in Safe Havens From Uncertain Tariff Impacts
data.description:
By Emese Bartha

Global government bonds extend their rally into the weekend in the aftermath of U.S. President Trump's tariff announcements and ahead of key payrolls data.

The 10-year Treasury yield slipped below 4% for the first time since October in overnight trade, continuing to trade below this level in mid-morning European hours. It last traded 10 basis points lower at 3.955%, according to Tradeweb.

European bonds take cue from Treasurys, with yields falling both inside and outside the eurozone.

The 10-year German Bund yield fell 9.5 basis points to a one-month low of 2.540%, pulling along eurozone peers. The 10-year U.K. gilt yield and the equivalent Swedish bond yields fell more than that, down about 12 basis points at 4.397% and 15 basis points at 2.449% respectively, according to Tradeweb data.

"Rates markets trade with a bullish tilt in the wake of tariffs," ING Research's senior rates strategist Benjamin Schroeder said in a note.

The U.S. tariff announcements on Wednesday brought some clarity but not an all-clear as trade negotiations continue and uncertainty itself may already be enough to weigh on sentiment, he said.

The U.S. tariffs look more aggressive than feared, raising recession fears among investors but also prompting concerns that inflation will take longer to fall back to central banks' targets.

"There is a sense that now the U.S. tariff announcements are behind us, so as newsflow settles, we may hope that market volatility may abate," BlueBay Asset Management's Chief Investment Officer Mark Dowding said in a note.

"Once this appears to be the case, we may have attractive entry levels to add to short rates positions, in anticipation that central banks will push back against market expectations of forthcoming monetary easing," he said.

Generali Investments shares the view that peak uncertainty is probably behind us, even as uncertainty will remain strong.

"We see euro area yields bottoming by the summer, while U.S. Treasury [yields] may have further scope to decrease," said senior economist Paolo Zanghieri in a note.

In the immediate near term, markets await U.S. payrolls data at 1230 GMT on Friday as a potential mover for U.S. Treasury yields. Analysts in The Wall Street Journal's poll forecast an addition of 140,000 people in March, slightly below February's 151,000.

However, the outcome might be weaker than the consensus, according to ING.

"A stronger jobs market will likely have less impact than a confirmation of weakening prospects that still have room to morph into a recession view. And for today our economist does see the risk of a weaker jobs report than the consensus," ING's Schroeder said.

Write to Emese Bartha at emese.bartha@wsj.com

data.title: Bitcoin, XRP, Other Crypto Prices Are Under Pressure. How Far They Could Fall.
data.description:
By Elsa Ohlen

Bitcoin, XRP and other cryptocurrencies climbed off their recent lows early Friday, even as U.S. stock futures continued to drop. Yet, the future for digital assets is looking uncertain.

The largest cryptocurrency Bitcoin traded modestly up early Friday, rising 0.9% to $84,445 over the last 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data. XRP, the digital coin used to facilitate and settle payments on Ripple's payment platform, rose 2.2% to $2.09.

Meanwhile, futures tracking the S&P 500 were down about 0.5%, after the benchmark index plunged 4.8% Thursday.

Bitcoin dropped below $82,000 Thursday, amid a sharp decline in stocks. The three main indexes had their worst day in five years after President Donald Trump announced higher-than-expected reciprocal tariffs on countries all over the world.

"Retaliatory tariffs, potential provocations and verbal interventions will be an excellent basis for increasing volatility in the cryptocurrency market," Tracy Jin, COO of crypto exchange MEXC, said.

A slowdown in the U.S. economy due to tariffs could also push the Federal Reserve to resume the interest rate cutting cycle, which, combined with the fall in Treasury yields and the weakening dollar, has a restraining effect on cryptocurrency and indexes, Jin added.

Bitcoin may end April in the $76,000 — $78,000 range, with a potential drop to $52,000 — $56,000 during the summer, according to Jin.

Among other cryptos, Ether traded flat at $1,815 and Solana slid 1% to $118. Memecoin Dogecoin gained 0.7%.

Write to Elsa Ohlen at elsa.ohlen@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Ningxia Orient Tantalum Industry Chairman Steps Down
data.description:
Ningxia Orient Tantalum Industry Chairman Wang Kai resigned, citing work adjustments, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The Chinese tantalum producer has yet to name Wang's successor.

data.title: Flydubai postpones launch of Skopje-Dubai flights - report
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), April 4 (SeeNews) - Flydubai is postponing the launch of a direct service from North Macedonia's Skopje to Dubai, local media reported on Friday.

The air carrier notified North Macedonia's transport ministry that it will delay the launch of flights to Skopje due to "objective reasons beyond the airline's control", news provider Sakam Da Kazham reported, citing a statement from the transport ministry.

The ministry did not specify when the airline will start operating the new service.

Flydubai planned to launch the flights on March 30 with financial support from North Macedonia's government as part of its open call for flight subsidies.

Passenger traffic at the international airports in Skopje and Ohrid reached 3,174,484 in 2024, an increase of 0.8% compared to the previous year, Aeroports de Paris (ADP) has said earlier.

data.title: Baffled Lesotho seeks to engage with US on 'shocking' tariffs
data.description:
Lesotho scrambled to put together a delegation on Friday to head to Washington to engage with the United States on tariffs that risk wiping out nearly half of its exports, its trade minister said, in what could be a death blow to its economy.

The 50% reciprocal trade tariff on the tiny southern African mountain kingdom was the highest levy on U.S. President Donald Trump's list of target economies.

"The latest policy direction undertaken by the United States is shocking ... as (it) ... has been a very important market for Lesotho," Trade Minister Mokhethi Shelile told parliament on Friday, adding that the 45% of exports went to the United States.

He said that officials had already engaged the U.S. embassy "to clarify and how, why Lesotho was included in the list of ... such high reciprocal tariffs".

Trump on Wednesday hit America's global trading partners with tariffs, upending decades of rules-based trade that campaigners have long said is exceptionally favourable to rich countries like the United States.

Lesotho's exports to the United States, mostly textiles for popular brands such as Levis, added up to $237 million in 2024 and account for more than a tenth of its GDP.

"Lesotho is also assembling a high-level delegation to the United States to try to maintain the current market dispensation," Shelile said.

In the medium term, he said, the kingdom would "increase efforts to export to alternative markets such as the European Union and the Africa free continental trade area".

Encircled by a South African mountain range, the kingdom of 2 million people is one of the world's poorest countries, with GDP per capita of $916 in 2023, according to World Bank figures.

data.title: Daiwa Securities Adjusts Price Target on Citigroup to $83 From $96, Keeps Buy Rating
